Our care

Personalised care for every stage of life

What we offer

Care built around you

At Flannery's, every care plan is individual. We take time to understand each resident's needs, preferences, and routines before they even arrive, so that from day one, it feels like home.

01

Long-term residential care

For residents who require full-time care and support, our long-term residential programme provides a permanent, safe, and homely environment. Each resident has their own room, personalised care plan, and a dedicated team.

We accept Fair Deal funded placements, private placements, and placements funded by other organisations.

Enquire about a placement
02

Respite care

Respite care provides a short-term stay for individuals who need temporary care, or to give family carers a well-deserved break. Our respite residents receive the exact same standard of care as our long-term residents.

We accept TCF-funded short-term care, private short-term, and other organisation-funded placements.

Enquire about respite
03

Dementia care

We have significant experience in supporting residents living with dementia. Our approach is built on consistency, patience, and deep respect for the individual — understanding that behind every diagnosis is a unique person with their own life story.

Our team receive ongoing training in dementia care, and care plans are reviewed regularly to adapt to changing needs.

Speak with our team
Fair Deal

Understanding the Fair Deal scheme

The Nursing Homes Support Scheme (Fair Deal) helps fund long-term residential care. Here's how it works in three steps.

1

Apply to the HSE

Apply online or through your local HSE office. A financial assessment determines your contribution. The HSE pays the rest.

2

Choose your home

Once approved, you choose your nursing home. Flannery's is an approved Fair Deal provider. Contact us to check availability.

3

Move in

We handle the paperwork with the HSE. Our admissions team guides you through every step to make the transition as smooth as possible.

Start an enquiry
